channel.title: Bash on Sysadmin - Linux administration, security and hardening
channel.description: Recent content in Bash on Sysadmin - Linux administration, security and hardening

item.title: Server basic configuration after installing RHEL 8.5 (minimal).
item.description: I saved it as script.sh, gave it chmod u+x script.sh executable permissions and ran it as root ./script.sh, although I know that scripts should be run from sudo for security. But I know what I’m doing and why. Everything is described with comments in English. User was created during installation of the system, hence the change. I use sudo and user on a daily basis. Here it is a quick solution.
item.dc:creator: sysadmin

item.title: Bash script that blocks web server (apache/nginx/litespeed) scanners.
item.description: Bash script that blocks web server (apache/nginx/litespeed) scanners Bash script that blocks web server (apache/nginx/litespeed) scanners. It checks the 400-408 errors or any other in the log you will choose to scan and extracts IP addresses of scanners which are trying to scan a web server and adds IP addresses to the ipset which drops the connection. #!/bin/bash ######################################################### Apache access_log ################################################################### #Look for the file and if does not exist create it.
item.dc:creator: sysadmin
